title_en: Methodology: The Transmitted Approach to Creed
content_en:
Al-Lalika'i's methodological approach embodies what is often called the athari or naqli (transmitted) method in Islamic theology, as distinct from the kalam or aqli (rational) method associated with the Ash'ari and Maturidi schools. Understanding this distinction is essential for situating Sharh Usul I'tiqad within the landscape of Islamic theological literature.

The transmitted method holds that creed is essentially a matter of receiving and preserving what God has revealed and what the Prophet and his companions taught. The proper activity of a theologian working in this tradition is not to construct doctrines through independent philosophical reasoning but to gather and transmit the teaching of authoritative predecessors, and to show that a particular creedal position is supported by an unbroken chain of transmission from the Prophet through the companions and successors. Rational arguments may be used to defend positions and to expose weaknesses in opposing views, but they do not generate doctrine; they serve positions already established by transmitted evidence.

This method has both advantages and limitations. Its strength is fidelity to the actual transmitted content of the religion and resistance to innovation driven by philosophical fashions. Al-Lalika'i's massive collection demonstrates that the creedal positions he documents were not invented by later scholars but were consistently held across the first centuries of Islamic history. This is genuinely valuable historical evidence that the positions are not retroactive constructions.

Its limitation, as critics from the kalam tradition pointed out, is that it does not always engage directly with the philosophical challenges that prompted the development of kalam theology in the first place. If a student raised on Greek philosophy argued that the divine attributes as affirmed in the traditionalist position implied anthropomorphism, a response consisting primarily of accumulated transmitted reports might not engage with the philosophical argument at the level it required.

Al-Lalika'i was aware of this limitation and addressed it in a way characteristic of the traditionalist approach: he argued that the philosophical challenges themselves arose from alien frameworks imported into Islamic thought, and that the proper response was to refuse the framework rather than to engage on its terms. The accumulated evidence from the early community showed that the traditionalist position was not naive but represented the considered understanding of scholars who were well aware of the philosophical traditions of their time.

The work's methodological approach made it particularly valuable for later scholars engaged in defending the Athari position. Ibn Taymiyyah, who cited Sharh Usul I'tiqad extensively in his own works, relied on it as a source base for demonstrating the historical depth of the Athari creedal positions. For Ibn Taymiyyah, the fact that these positions could be documented across the first three centuries of Islamic history was itself a theological argument: the community's consensus, preserved in thousands of transmitted reports, was not to be lightly dismissed in favor of the conclusions of later philosophers.
content_id:
Pendekatan metodologis al-Lalika'i mewujudkan apa yang sering disebut metode athari atau naqli (riwayat) dalam teologi Islam, berbeda dari metode kalam atau aqli (rasional) yang terkait dengan mazhab Asy'ari dan Maturidi. Memahami perbedaan ini sangat penting untuk menempatkan Sharh Usul I'tiqad dalam lanskap literatur teologi Islam.

Metode riwayat berpendapat bahwa akidah pada dasarnya adalah masalah menerima dan melestarikan apa yang Allah wahyukan dan apa yang Nabi ﷺ serta para sahabatnya ajarkan. Aktivitas yang tepat bagi seorang teolog yang bekerja dalam tradisi ini bukan untuk mengkonstruksi doktrin melalui penalaran filosofis independen melainkan untuk mengumpulkan dan menyampaikan pengajaran para pendahulu yang otoritatif, dan untuk menunjukkan bahwa posisi akidah tertentu didukung oleh rantai periwayatan yang tidak terputus dari Nabi ﷺ melalui para sahabat dan tabi'in. Argumen-argumen rasional dapat digunakan untuk membela posisi-posisi dan mengekspos kelemahan-kelemahan dalam pandangan-pandangan yang berlawanan, tetapi argumen-argumen tersebut tidak menghasilkan doktrin; argumen-argumen itu melayani posisi-posisi yang sudah ditetapkan oleh bukti riwayat.

Metode ini memiliki kelebihan maupun keterbatasan. Kelebihannya adalah kesetiaan kepada konten riwayat agama yang sesungguhnya dan resistensi terhadap inovasi yang didorong oleh mode-mode filosofis. Koleksi besar al-Lalika'i menunjukkan bahwa posisi-posisi akidah yang beliau dokumentasikan bukan diciptakan oleh ulama-ulama kemudian melainkan secara konsisten dipegang sepanjang abad-abad pertama sejarah Islam. Ini adalah bukti historis yang benar-benar berharga bahwa posisi-posisi tersebut bukan konstruksi retroaktif.

Keterbatasannya, sebagaimana dikritik oleh para sarjana dari tradisi kalam, adalah bahwa metode ini tidak selalu terlibat secara langsung dengan tantangan-tantangan filosofis yang mendorong perkembangan teologi kalam sejak awal. Jika seorang murid yang besar dengan filsafat Yunani berargumen bahwa sifat-sifat ilahiah sebagaimana ditegaskan dalam posisi tradisionalis mengimplikasikan antropomorfisme, sebuah respons yang terutama terdiri dari akumulasi laporan-laporan riwayat mungkin tidak terlibat dengan argumen filosofis pada tingkat yang diperlukan.

Al-Lalika'i menyadari keterbatasan ini dan mengatasinya dengan cara yang khas dari pendekatan tradisionalis: beliau berargumen bahwa tantangan-tantangan filosofis itu sendiri berasal dari kerangka-kerangka asing yang diimpor ke dalam pemikiran Islam, dan bahwa respons yang tepat adalah menolak kerangka tersebut daripada terlibat dengan syarat-syaratnya. Bukti yang terkumpul dari komunitas awal menunjukkan bahwa posisi tradisionalis bukan naif melainkan mewakili pemahaman yang dipertimbangkan dari para ulama yang sangat menyadari tradisi-tradisi filosofis pada zamannya.

Pendekatan metodologis karya ini menjadikannya sangat berharga bagi ulama-ulama kemudian yang terlibat dalam membela posisi Athari. Ibn Taymiyyah, yang mengutip Sharh Usul I'tiqad secara ekstensif dalam karya-karyanya sendiri, mengandalkannya sebagai basis sumber untuk menunjukkan kedalaman historis posisi-posisi akidah Athari. Bagi Ibn Taymiyyah, fakta bahwa posisi-posisi ini dapat didokumentasikan sepanjang tiga abad pertama sejarah Islam itu sendiri adalah argumen teologis: konsensus komunitas, yang tersimpan dalam ribuan laporan riwayat, tidak boleh dengan mudah dikesampingkan demi kesimpulan-kesimpulan para filsuf kemudian.
content_ar: Al-Lalika'i's methodological approach embodies what is often called the الأثري or naqli (transmitted) method in Islamic theology, as distinct from the علم الكلام or aqli (rational) method associated with the الأشعري and الماتريدي schools. الفهم this distinction is الجوهري for situating Sharh الأصول I'tiqad within the landscape of Islamic theological literature. The transmitted method holds that creed is في جوهره a matter of receiving and preserving what God has revealed and what النبي ﷺ and his الصحابة taught. The proper activity of a theologian working in this التراث is not to construct doctrines through independent philosophical reasoning but to gather and transmit the teaching of المعتمد predecessors, and to show that a particular creedal position is supported by an unbroken chain of transmission from النبي ﷺ through the الصحابة and التابعين. Rational arguments may be used to defend positions and to expose weaknesses in opposing views, but they do not generate doctrine; they serve positions already established by transmitted evidence. This method has both advantages and limitations. Its القوة is fidelity to the actual transmitted content of the religion and resistance to البدعة driven by philosophical fashions. Al-Lalika'i's massive collection demonstrates that the creedal positions he documents were not invented by later العلماء but were consistently held across the أولاً centuries of Islamic history. This is genuinely valuable historical evidence that the positions are not retroactive constructions. Its limitation, as critics from the علم الكلام التراث pointed out, is that it does not always engage directly with the philosophical challenges that prompted the development of علم الكلام theology in the أولاً place. إذا a student raised on Greek philosophy argued that the divine attributes as affirmed in the traditionalist position implied anthropomorphism, a response consisting primarily of accumulated transmitted reports might not engage with the philosophical argument at the level it required. Al-Lalika'i was aware of this limitation and addressed it in a الطريق characteristic of the traditionalist approach: احتج that the philosophical challenges themselves arose from alien frameworks imported into Islamic thought, and that the proper response was to refuse the framework rather than to engage on its terms. The accumulated evidence from the early الجماعة showed that the traditionalist position was not naive but represented the considered الفهم of العلماء who were well aware of the philosophical traditions of their time. The work's methodological approach made it particularly valuable for later العلماء engaged in defending the الأثري position. ابن تيمية, who cited Sharh الأصول I'tiqad extensively in his own works, relied on it as a source base for demonstrating the historical depth of the الأثري creedal positions.
